content: DSM-5-TR criteria: Anorexia—energy restriction leading to significantly low weight (context-dependent), intense fear of gaining weight/becoming fat, disturbed body image/undue influence of weight on self-evaluation/denial of seriousness. Subtypes: restricting vs binge-eating/purging. Bulimia—recurrent binge eating (large amount + loss of control) and compensatory behaviors ≥1/week for ≥3 months, self-evaluation unduly influenced by shape/weight. BED—recurrent binge eating ≥1/week for ≥3 months without compensatory behaviors, marked distress. Severity specifiers based on BMI (anorexia) or episode frequency (bulimia/BED). Amenorrhea no longer required for anorexia diagnosis.
